response.justification: Strong senior-level engineer with proven systems thinking, architectural rigor, and IDE/tooling experience, but significant technical skill gaps for this role. Java expertise is over a decade old and lacks any Kotlin experience; recent 5+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js rather than JVM. Missing demonstrated experience with Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, and other enterprise frameworks that are core to the position. Could succeed with substantial ramp-up, but hiring risk is elevated for a specialized IDE tooling role.
strengths: Senior-level systems engineer with deep experience at scale—microservices handling millions of requests/second, performance optimization, and complex architectural design
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, clean code, and extensible design; authored multiple testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Hands-on IDE and language tooling experience (LabWindows/CVI C IDE at National Instruments) with genuine interest in developer-facing systems
concerns: No Kotlin experience and Java expertise is outdated (10+ years since National Instruments role); recent work heavily skews toward TypeScript/Node.js
No demonstrated experience with Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java frameworks—all core to this role
IDE internals experience is domain-specific to C tooling rather than Java/Kotlin ecosystem; gap in modern JVM IDE architecture knowledge
